methods: STBLLM: Structured Binarization for LLMs to achieve <1-bit precision
N:M sparsity technique for structural binarization of weights
Standardized Importance (SI) metric for weight significance assessment
Layerwise adaptive N:M binarization (different N:M ratios per layer)
Fine-grained grouping strategy for non-salient weights with trisection search for optimal splitting and region-specific quantization
Residual approximation for salient parameters
Specialized CUDA kernel for structural binarization leveraging NVIDIA Ampere GPU sparse tensor cores
problems: Memory and computational inefficiency of large language models (LLMs) during inference, especially on resource-constrained devices
Limitations of 1-bit binarization for LLMs (redundancy and insufficient compression)
Need for further compression of LLMs below 1-bit per weight without significant performance loss
metrics: Speedup (inference speed, e.g., x17.85 over ABQ-LLM)
novelty_claims: First structural binarization method for LLMs achieving compression to less than 1-bit per weight
Introduction of the Standardized Importance (SI) metric for efficient and accurate weight significance assessment in binarization
Adaptive layerwise N:M structured binarization for balancing compression and accuracy
Fine-grained grouping and region-specific quantization for non-salient weights in LLMs
Specialized CUDA kernel for efficient structural binarization on NVIDIA Ampere GPUs
Demonstrates that LLMs can be compressed below 1-bit per weight with minimal performance loss, outperforming previous binarization methods

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["BiLLM: a 1-bit post-training quantization (PTQ) scheme for LLMs","Hessian-guided structural selection of salient weights","Binary residual approximation for salient weights","Optimal splitting search and binarization for non-salient weights based on bell-shaped distribution","Block-wise error compensation"],"problems":["Maintaining LLM performance under ultra-low bit-width (1-bit) quantization","Reducing memory and computation requirements for large language models (LLMs)","Overcoming performance collapse of existing PTQ methods at ≤3 bits for LLMs","Enabling efficient and accurate binarization of LLMs for deployment on resource-constrained devices"],"datasets":["WikiText2"],"metrics":["Perplexity","Bit-width (average bits per weight)","Quantization time (hours)"],"results":[{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA2-70B, WikiText2)","value":"8.41 with 1.08-bit weights"},{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA-65B, WikiText2)","value":"8.49 with 1.08-bit weights"},{"metric":"Perplexity (OPT-66B, FP16, WikiText2)","value":"9.34"},{"metric":"Bit-width (average)","value":"1.07 ~ 1.11 bits"},{"metric":"Quantization time (7B weights, 1 GPU)","value":"0.5 hours"}],"novelty_claims":["First 1-bit post-training quantization (PTQ) scheme for LLMs achieving high-accuracy inference","Novel Hessian-guided structural selection and binary residual approximation for salient weights","Optimal splitting and binarization strategy for non-salient weights based on bell-shaped distribution","Achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in 1-bit quantization, outperforming previous methods by significant margins","Enables binarization of LLMs with billions of weights in under an hour on a single GPU"]}

methods: BiLLM: a 1-bit post-training quantization (PTQ) scheme for LLMs
Hessian-guided structural selection of salient weights
Binary residual approximation for salient weights
Optimal splitting search and binarization for non-salient weights based on bell-shaped distribution
Block-wise error compensation
problems: Maintaining LLM performance under ultra-low bit-width (1-bit) quantization
Reducing memory and computation requirements for large language models (LLMs)
Overcoming performance collapse of existing PTQ methods at ≤3 bits for LLMs
Enabling efficient and accurate binarization of LLMs for deployment on resource-constrained devices
novelty_claims: First 1-bit post-training quantization (PTQ) scheme for LLMs achieving high-accuracy inference
Novel Hessian-guided structural selection and binary residual approximation for salient weights
Optimal splitting and binarization strategy for non-salient weights based on bell-shaped distribution
Achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in 1-bit quantization, outperforming previous methods by significant margins
Enables binarization of LLMs with billions of weights in under an hour on a single GPU

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Omnidirectionally calibrated Quantization (OmniQuant)","Learnable Weight Clipping (LWC)","Learnable Equivalent Transformation (LET)","Block-wise error minimization with differentiable optimization"],"problems":["Efficient post-training quantization (PTQ) of large language models (LLMs) with minimal performance degradation, especially in low-bit quantization settings","Reducing memory and computational requirements for LLM deployment","Overcoming the limitations of hand-crafted quantization parameters in existing PTQ methods","Achieving QAT-level performance with PTQ-level efficiency"],"datasets":["LLaMA-2 model family (7B-70B)","OPT","LLaMA","LLaMA-2-chat","Falcon"],"metrics":["Perplexity","Accuracy","Inference speed","Memory reduction"],"results":[{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA-13B, W2A16)","value":"13.21 (OmniQuant) vs 3832 (GPTQ)"},{"metric":"Accuracy (W4A4, 128 samples, 1.6h)","value":"46.43%"},{"metric":"Accuracy (W4A4, 100k samples, 90h, LLM-QAT)","value":"52.65%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes OmniQuant, a novel quantization pipeline for LLMs that freezes full-precision weights and introduces a small set of learnable quantization parameters, enabling gradient-based updates while maintaining PTQ efficiency.","Introduces Learnable Weight Clipping (LWC) to optimize weight clipping thresholds and Learnable Equivalent Transformation (LET) to address activation outliers, both integrated in a differentiable framework.","Achieves state-of-the-art performance in low-bit quantization settings (e.g., W4A4, W2A16) with significantly reduced time and data requirements compared to QAT-based methods.","Demonstrates versatility across both weight-only and weight-activation quantization, and across a wide range of LLM architectures and sizes.","OmniQuant introduces no extra computation or parameters for the quantized model, as the learned parameters are fused into the quantized weights."]}

methods: Omnidirectionally calibrated Quantization (OmniQuant)
Learnable Weight Clipping (LWC)
Learnable Equivalent Transformation (LET)
Block-wise error minimization with differentiable optimization
problems: Efficient post-training quantization (PTQ) of large language models (LLMs) with minimal performance degradation, especially in low-bit quantization settings
Reducing memory and computational requirements for LLM deployment
Overcoming the limitations of hand-crafted quantization parameters in existing PTQ methods
Achieving QAT-level performance with PTQ-level efficiency
novelty_claims: Proposes OmniQuant, a novel quantization pipeline for LLMs that freezes full-precision weights and introduces a small set of learnable quantization parameters, enabling gradient-based updates while maintaining PTQ efficiency.

Introduces Learnable Weight Clipping (LWC) to optimize weight clipping thresholds and Learnable Equivalent Transformation (LET) to address activation outliers, both integrated in a differentiable framework.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance in low-bit quantization settings (e.g., W4A4, W2A16) with significantly reduced time and data requirements compared to QAT-based methods.
Demonstrates versatility across both weight-only and weight-activation quantization, and across a wide range of LLM architectures and sizes.
OmniQuant introduces no extra computation or parameters for the quantized model, as the learned parameters are fused into the quantized weights.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["OneBit: a 1-bit model compressing framework for LLMs","Novel 1-bit parameter representation method for quantizing LLMs","Sign-Value-Independent Decomposition (SVID) for weight matrices","Parameter initialization method based on matrix decomposition (SVID)","Quantization-aware knowledge distillation for transferring capabilities from full-precision to 1-bit models"],"problems":["Severe performance degradation of LLMs when quantized to extremely low bit-width (1-bit)","High computational and memory overheads of deploying large language models (LLMs)","Ineffectiveness of existing quantization methods at 1-bit weight representation"],"datasets":["Wikitext2","OPT (1.3B to 13B)","LLaMA (1.3B to 13B)","LLaMA2"],"metrics":["Perplexity","Performance relative to non-quantized model (percentage)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance relative to non-quantized model (LLaMA)","value":"at least 81%"},{"metric":"Perplexity (Wikitext2, LLaMA-7B)","value":"1-bit method outperforms 2-bit baselines (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to successfully quantize LLM weight matrices to 1-bit with robust performance.","Proposes a novel 1-bit parameter representation using a sign matrix and two value vectors for each weight matrix.","Introduces Sign-Value-Independent Decomposition (SVID) for effective parameter initialization and improved convergence.","Demonstrates that 1-bit quantized LLMs can achieve at least 81% of the non-quantized performance on LLaMA models.","Shows generalizability across multiple LLM architectures and sizes (OPT, LLaMA, LLaMA2, 1.3B to 13B)."]}

methods: OneBit: a 1-bit model compressing framework for LLMs
Novel 1-bit parameter representation method for quantizing LLMs
Sign-Value-Independent Decomposition (SVID) for weight matrices
Parameter initialization method based on matrix decomposition (SVID)
Quantization-aware knowledge distillation for transferring capabilities from full-precision to 1-bit models
problems: Severe performance degradation of LLMs when quantized to extremely low bit-width (1-bit)
High computational and memory overheads of deploying large language models (LLMs)
Ineffectiveness of existing quantization methods at 1-bit weight representation

results.value: 1-bit method outperforms 2-bit baselines (exact value not specified)

novelty_claims: First to successfully quantize LLM weight matrices to 1-bit with robust performance.
Proposes a novel 1-bit parameter representation using a sign matrix and two value vectors for each weight matrix.
Introduces Sign-Value-Independent Decomposition (SVID) for effective parameter initialization and improved convergence.
Demonstrates that 1-bit quantized LLMs can achieve at least 81% of the non-quantized performance on LLaMA models.
Shows generalizability across multiple LLM architectures and sizes (OPT, LLaMA, LLaMA2, 1.3B to 13B).

methods: Arbitrary-bit quantization algorithm for LLMs (ABQ-LLM)
Block-wise distribution correction and compensation for transformer blocks
Bit balance strategy for low-bit (e.g., INT2) quantization
Double cosine similarity distribution correction and attention map distribution bootstrap for sensitive layers
Custom quantization acceleration framework using BTC (Binary TensorCore) equivalents for arbitrary precision matrix multiplication
Software engine for fully quantized inference with arbitrary precision combinations
problems: Performance degradation in LLMs due to low-bit quantization
Inefficient acceleration of quantized matrix operations with arbitrary precisions on GPUs due to hardware limitations (restricted to INT4/INT8)
Distribution differences and asymmetric loss in full quantization of weights and activations, especially at low bit-widths
Inefficiency in GEMV operations and memory access during LLM inference

results.metric: Perplexity reduction vs AffineQuant (LLaMA-7B, W2A8, WikiText2)
Memory compression gain vs SmoothQuant (LLaMA-7B, W2A8)

novelty_claims: First quantization framework for LLMs enabling efficient arbitrary-bit (arbitrary-precision) quantized inference on GPU, overcoming INT4/INT8 hardware limitations.
Introduction of a block-wise distribution correction and compensation scheme to mitigate distribution discrepancies from full quantization, improving low-bit performance.
Proposal of the bit balance strategy to address asymmetric loss in INT2 quantization, significantly improving performance at very low bit-widths.
Development of a custom software engine that achieves quantization freedom, supporting fully quantized inference for any precision combination and maximizing acceleration and memory efficiency.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Partially-Binarized LLM (PB-LLM): selectively binarizing weights while storing a small ratio of salient weights in higher-bit precision","Salient weight detection and filtering for partial binarization","Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) for PB-LLM: reconstructing binarized weight matrices guided by the Hessian matrix (inspired by GPTQ)","Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) for PB-LLM: freezing salient weights and deriving optimal scaling factors for residual binarized weights"],"problems":["Extreme quantization of large language models (LLMs) to reduce memory and storage requirements","Maintaining linguistic reasoning capacity and performance of LLMs under aggressive (1-bit) quantization/binarization","Overcoming the collapse of LLM performance with naive binarization methods"],"datasets":["BoolQ"],"metrics":["Accuracy (on BoolQ)"],"results":[{"metric":"Accuracy (BoolQ, PB-LLM-OA615)","value":"0.605"},{"metric":"Accuracy (BoolQ, Network FP OPT1.3B)","value":"0.60"},{"metric":"Accuracy (BoolQ, Partially-Binarized)","value":"0.550"},{"metric":"Accuracy (BoolQ, Partially-Binarized Activation)","value":"0.540"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose partial binarization for LLMs, storing only a small ratio of salient weights in higher precision to enable extreme low-bit quantization while preserving reasoning capacity.","Demonstrates that naive binarization fails for LLMs and highlights the importance of salient weights for successful low-bit quantization.","Introduces a PTQ method for PB-LLM using Hessian-guided reconstruction (inspired by GPTQ) to recover reasoning capacity in low-bit models.","Proposes a QAT method for PB-LLM, including freezing salient weights and deriving optimal scaling factors for binarized weights, to minimize quantization error and improve performance.","Shows that PB-LLM achieves comparable reasoning capacity to full-precision models at extremely low bit-widths."]}

methods: Partially-Binarized LLM (PB-LLM): selectively binarizing weights while storing a small ratio of salient weights in higher-bit precision
Salient weight detection and filtering for partial binarization
Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) for PB-LLM: reconstructing binarized weight matrices guided by the Hessian matrix (inspired by GPTQ)
Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) for PB-LLM: freezing salient weights and deriving optimal scaling factors for residual binarized weights
problems: Extreme quantization of large language models (LLMs) to reduce memory and storage requirements
Maintaining linguistic reasoning capacity and performance of LLMs under aggressive (1-bit) quantization/binarization
Overcoming the collapse of LLM performance with naive binarization methods
novelty_claims: First to propose partial binarization for LLMs, storing only a small ratio of salient weights in higher precision to enable extreme low-bit quantization while preserving reasoning capacity.
Demonstrates that naive binarization fails for LLMs and highlights the importance of salient weights for successful low-bit quantization.
Introduces a PTQ method for PB-LLM using Hessian-guided reconstruction (inspired by GPTQ) to recover reasoning capacity in low-bit models.
Proposes a QAT method for PB-LLM, including freezing salient weights and deriving optimal scaling factors for binarized weights, to minimize quantization error and improve performance.
Shows that PB-LLM achieves comparable reasoning capacity to full-precision models at extremely low bit-widths.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Dual-Binarization (DB-LLM)","Flexible Dual Binarization (FDB)","Deviation-Aware Distillation (DAD)"],"problems":["Accuracy degradation in ultra-low-bit (≤2-bit) quantization of large language models (LLMs)","Inefficiency and poor representation capability of binarized weights in LLMs","Distortion and bias in prediction preferences of low-bit LLMs, especially on ambiguous samples","High memory and computation consumption of LLMs, impeding deployment on resource-limited devices"],"datasets":["WikiText2","LLaMA-1-30B","LLaMA-1-65B","LLaMA-1-7B","LLaMA-2 family (implied)"],"metrics":["Perplexity","Computational consumption (reduction %)"],"results":[{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA-1-30B, 2-bit)","value":"5.52"},{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA-1-65B, 2-bit)","value":"4.84"},{"metric":"Perplexity (LLaMA-1-7B, full-precision)","value":"5.68"},{"metric":"Perplexity (SOTA, 2-bit)","value":"9.64"},{"metric":"Perplexity (DB-LLM, 2-bit)","value":"7.23"},{"metric":"Computational consumption reduction vs SOTA (same bit-width)","value":"20%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel Dual-Binarization method (DB-LLM) for accurate and efficient 2-bit quantization of LLMs.","Introduces Flexible Dual Binarization (FDB), which splits 2-bit quantized weights into two independent binaries, maximizing representation while leveraging binary operation efficiency.","Presents Deviation-Aware Distillation (DAD) to mitigate prediction distortion in low-bit LLMs by re-weighting distillation loss based on sample ambiguity.","DB-LLM achieves state-of-the-art perplexity and computational efficiency in ultra-low-bit quantization, outperforming previous methods."]}

methods: Dual-Binarization (DB-LLM)
Flexible Dual Binarization (FDB)
Deviation-Aware Distillation (DAD)
problems: Accuracy degradation in ultra-low-bit (≤2-bit) quantization of large language models (LLMs)
Inefficiency and poor representation capability of binarized weights in LLMs
Distortion and bias in prediction preferences of low-bit LLMs, especially on ambiguous samples
High memory and computation consumption of LLMs, impeding deployment on resource-limited devices

results.metric: Computational consumption reduction vs SOTA (same bit-width)

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel Dual-Binarization method (DB-LLM) for accurate and efficient 2-bit quantization of LLMs.
Introduces Flexible Dual Binarization (FDB), which splits 2-bit quantized weights into two independent binaries, maximizing representation while leveraging binary operation efficiency.
Presents Deviation-Aware Distillation (DAD) to mitigate prediction distortion in low-bit LLMs by re-weighting distillation loss based on sample ambiguity.
DB-LLM achieves state-of-the-art perplexity and computational efficiency in ultra-low-bit quantization, outperforming previous methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Sub-bit Neural Networks (SNNs): a new binary quantization design that compresses and accelerates Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) by learning to use subsets of binary kernels per layer","Kernel-aware optimization framework: exploits binary quantization in the fine-grained convolutional kernel space","Random sampling of layer-specific subsets of the kernel space","Refinement step: learning to adjust the subsets of binary kernels via optimization"],"problems":["Further compressing and accelerating Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) beyond 1-bit per weight while maintaining accuracy","Reducing storage and computational costs of deep neural networks for deployment on resource-constrained devices","Identifying and leveraging clustered distributions of binary kernels in BNNs for more efficient quantization"],"datasets":["ImageNet","CIFAR-10"],"metrics":["Runtime speedup","Compression ratio","Recognition accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Runtime speedup (ResNet-18 on ImageNet)","value":"3.13× over BNN"},{"metric":"Runtime speedup (ResNet-34 on ImageNet)","value":"3.33× over BNN"},{"metric":"Compression ratio (ResNet-18/ResNet-34 on ImageNet)","value":"1.8× over BNN"},{"metric":"Bit-width per weight (SNN)","value":"0.56-bit"},{"metric":"Recognition accuracy drop","value":"Moderate (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First method to simultaneously compress and accelerate BNNs in a quantization pipeline by going below 1-bit per weight","Proposes a kernel-aware optimization framework that leverages the clustered distribution of binary kernels in BNNs","Introduces a two-step process (random sampling and refinement) to learn optimal subsets of binary kernels per layer","Demonstrates that SNNs can achieve significant compression and acceleration over BNNs with only moderate accuracy loss"]}

methods: Sub-bit Neural Networks (SNNs): a new binary quantization design that compresses and accelerates Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) by learning to use subsets of binary kernels per layer
Kernel-aware optimization framework: exploits binary quantization in the fine-grained convolutional kernel space
Random sampling of layer-specific subsets of the kernel space
Refinement step: learning to adjust the subsets of binary kernels via optimization
problems: Further compressing and accelerating Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) beyond 1-bit per weight while maintaining accuracy
Reducing storage and computational costs of deep neural networks for deployment on resource-constrained devices
Identifying and leveraging clustered distributions of binary kernels in BNNs for more efficient quantization
novelty_claims: First method to simultaneously compress and accelerate BNNs in a quantization pipeline by going below 1-bit per weight
Proposes a kernel-aware optimization framework that leverages the clustered distribution of binary kernels in BNNs
Introduces a two-step process (random sampling and refinement) to learn optimal subsets of binary kernels per layer
Demonstrates that SNNs can achieve significant compression and acceleration over BNNs with only moderate accuracy loss

methods: Sparse-Quantized Representation (SpQR): a hybrid sparse-quantized format for LLM weight compression
Outlier weight detection and isolation: identifying weights that cause large quantization errors and storing them in higher precision
Grouped quantization with small group size and quantized scales
Extended posttraining quantization (PTQ) approach based on GPTQ, with layer-wise L2 error minimization
Specialized sparse-matrix multiplication algorithm based on compressed sparse row (CSR) format for efficient inference
problems: Significant accuracy loss in LLMs when quantized to 3-4 bits per parameter, especially for smaller models (1-10B parameters)
Need for near-lossless compression of LLMs to fit on memory-limited devices without degrading generative quality
Existing quantization methods causing cumulative errors in sequential LLM generation

results.value: 33B parameter LLM fits on 24GB consumer GPU without performance degradation

novelty_claims: First weight quantization method to achieve near-lossless compression (less than 1% relative perplexity loss) at 3-4 bits per parameter for LLMs across model scales
Novel outlier detection and isolation for weights, not just input features, to minimize quantization error
Efficient encoding and decoding algorithms for SpQR, including a specialized sparse-matrix multiplication for inference
Demonstration that output hidden dimension outliers exist and can be exploited for compression
SpQR enables running large LLMs (e.g., 33B parameters) on consumer GPUs with no performance degradation and significant speedup

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ): a hardware-friendly low-bit weight-only quantization method for LLMs that protects salient weights by observing activations and uses per-channel scaling to minimize quantization error without backpropagation or reconstruction.","TinyChat: an efficient inference framework for deploying 4-bit quantized LLMs on edge devices, featuring on-the-fly dequantization, efficient 4-bit weight packing, and kernel fusion."],"problems":["Reducing the memory footprint and accelerating inference of large language models (LLMs) for deployment on edge devices and resource-constrained hardware.","Maintaining high accuracy and generalization ability of LLMs under low-bit quantization without overfitting to calibration methods: Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ): a hardware-friendly low-bit weight-only quantization method for LLMs that protects salient weights by observing activations and uses per-channel scaling to minimize quantization error without backpropagation or reconstruction.
TinyChat: an efficient inference framework for deploying 4-bit quantized LLMs on edge devices, featuring on-the-fly dequantization, efficient 4-bit weight packing, and kernel fusion.
problems: Reducing the memory footprint and accelerating inference of large language models (LLMs) for deployment on edge devices and resource-constrained hardware.
Maintaining high accuracy and generalization ability of LLMs under low-bit quantization without overfitting to calibration sets or requiring expensive quantization-aware training.
Overcoming the hardware inefficiency and accuracy degradation of existing post-training quantization methods for LLMs.
metrics: Inference speedup (relative to FP16 and GPTQ)

results.value: 3.2-3.3x average speedup across desktop, laptop, and mobile GPUs

results.metric: Interactive pace for 13B LLM on RTX 4070 (8GB)

novelty_claims: AWQ introduces activation-aware weight quantization, which protects only a small fraction of salient weights by observing activations, not weights, for optimal per-channel scaling.
AWQ does not require backpropagation, reconstruction, or data layout reordering, preserving generalization and hardware efficiency.
AWQ achieves state-of-the-art quantization performance for instruction-tuned and, for the first time, multi-modal LLMs.
AWQ is implemented in TinyChat, enabling efficient, reorder-free online dequantization and practical deployment of large LLMs on edge devices with significant speedup and memory savings.
AWQ has been widely adopted by industry and open-source communities, demonstrating its practical impact.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Outlier Weighed Layerwise sparsity (OWL): a pruning method for LLMs that assigns non-uniform layerwise sparsity ratios proportional to the outlier ratio in each layer"],"problems":["Reducing the size and computational demands of Large Language Models (LLMs) for practical deployment","Improving pruning strategies for LLMs to achieve higher sparsity without significant performance degradation","Addressing the limitations of uniform layerwise sparsity in LLM pruning"],"datasets":["LLaMA-V1 family (7B to 65B)","OPT (7B to 66B)","WikiText"],"metrics":["Perplexity","End-to-end inference speedup (DeepSparse inference engine)"],"results":[{"metric":"Perplexity reduction over Wanda at 70% sparsity (LLaMA-7B on WikiText)","value":"61.22"},{"metric":"Perplexity reduction over Wanda at 80% sparsity (LLaMA-7B on WikiText)","value":"3300"},{"metric":"Perplexity reduction over SparseGPT at 70% sparsity","value":"6.80"},{"metric":"End-to-end inference speedup (DeepSparse, 70%-90% sparsity)","value":"2.6x - 3.9x"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose non-uniform layerwise sparsity for LLM pruning, challenging the conventional belief that uniform layerwise sparsity is optimal for LLMs","Introduces OWL, which aligns layerwise sparsity with the distribution of activation outliers, leading to better pruning performance","Demonstrates that non-uniform layerwise sparsity, guided by outlier ratios, enables higher one-shot pruning ratios (up to 70%) with minimal performance loss in LLMs","Provides empirical evidence that OWL outperforms state-of-the-art methods (Wanda, SparseGPT) in both perplexity and inference speedup"]}

methods: Outlier Weighed Layerwise sparsity (OWL): a pruning method for LLMs that assigns non-uniform layerwise sparsity ratios proportional to the outlier ratio in each layer
problems: Reducing the size and computational demands of Large Language Models (LLMs) for practical deployment

novelty_claims: First to propose non-uniform layerwise sparsity for LLM pruning, challenging the conventional belief that uniform layerwise sparsity is optimal for LLMs
Introduces OWL, which aligns layerwise sparsity with the distribution of activation outliers, leading to better pruning performance
Demonstrates that non-uniform layerwise sparsity, guided by outlier ratios, enables higher one-shot pruning ratios (up to 70%) with minimal performance loss in LLMs
Provides empirical evidence that OWL outperforms state-of-the-art methods (Wanda, SparseGPT) in both perplexity and inference speedup

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["QuIP (Quantization with Incoherence Processing): a two-step method involving (1) adaptive rounding minimizing a quadratic proxy objective using Hessian estimates, and (2) efficient pre- and post-processing via multiplication by random orthogonal matrices to ensure incoherence of weights and Hessians","Theoretical analysis of quantization algorithms for LLMs, including QuIP and OPTQ","Incoherence processing as a form of outlier suppression across weights and activation space"],"problems":["Post-training quantization of large language model (LLM) parameters to improve runtime efficiency","Achieving viable 2-bit quantization of LLMs without significant loss in performance","Lack of theoretical analysis for LLM-scale quantization algorithms"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Model accuracy (implied, as results are about 'viable results' and 'gaps' between quantized and original models)","Compression rate (bits per weight)"],"results":[{"metric":"Viable 2-bit quantization","value":"First LLM quantization method to produce viable results using only two bits per weight"},{"metric":"Gap between 2-bit and 4-bit compression","value":"Small gaps observed for models >2B parameters, which decrease with model size"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of QuIP, a new quantization method for LLMs based on incoherence processing","First theoretical analysis for a quantization algorithm that scales to LLM-sized models, including both QuIP and OPTQ","Demonstration that QuIP enables viable 2-bit quantization of LLMs for the first time","Empirical finding that incoherence processing improves several existing quantization algorithms"]}

Incoherence processing as a form of outlier suppression across weights and activation space
problems: Post-training quantization of large language model (LLM) parameters to improve runtime efficiency

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Outlier-aware weight quantization (OWQ): a post-training quantization method that identifies vulnerable weights based on activation outliers and allocates high-precision to them"],"problems":["Reducing inference cost and memory requirements of large language models via weight quantization with minimal quality degradation","Addressing errors in weight quantization caused by activation outliers"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Model quality (not specified, but likely perplexity or accuracy on language tasks)","Bit-width of quantized weights"],"results":[{"metric":"Model quality (3.01-bit OWQ vs 4-bit OPTQ)","value":"3.01-bit OWQ models exhibit comparable quality to 4-bit OPTQ models"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel post-training quantization (PTQ) method for weights that is outlier-aware, leveraging activation outlier analysis to identify vulnerable weights.","OWQ allocates high-precision to weights most affected by activation outliers, improving quantization quality at lower bit-widths.","Demonstrates that 3.01-bit OWQ models can match the quality of 4-bit OPTQ models, reducing memory and compute requirements further."]}

Addressing errors in weight quantization caused by activation outliers

results.value: 3.01-bit OWQ models exhibit comparable quality to 4-bit OPTQ models

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel post-training quantization (PTQ) method for weights that is outlier-aware, leveraging activation outlier analysis to identify vulnerable weights.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Wanda (Pruning by Weights and Activations): a pruning method that scores each weight by the product of its magnitude and the norm of the corresponding input activations, and prunes weights with the lowest scores on a per-output basis, without retraining or weight updates"],"problems":["Efficiently pruning large language models (LLMs) to reduce computational and memory requirements without significant loss in performance","Overcoming the limitations of existing pruning methods that require retraining or computationally expensive weight updates, which are impractical for billion-scale LLMs"],"datasets":["LLaMA","LLaMA-2"],"metrics":["Performance on language benchmarks (e.g., accuracy, perplexity, or other standard LLM evaluation metrics)","Sparsity level (percentage of weights pruned)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance retention at high sparsity (compared to magnitude pruning)","value":"Wanda significantly outperforms magnitude pruning at the same sparsity levels"},{"metric":"Performance compared to SparseGPT","value":"Wanda performs competitively with SparseGPT while requiring lower computational cost"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Wanda, a simple and effective pruning method for LLMs that does not require retraining or weight updates","Introduces a new pruning metric based on the product of weight magnitude and input activation norm, computed per-output rather than globally","Demonstrates that Wanda can induce high sparsity in LLMs with minimal performance loss and lower computational cost than prior methods"]}

novelty_claims: Proposes a three-stage compression pipeline (pruning, trained quantization, Huffman coding) that achieves very high compression rates without accuracy loss.

Shows that compressed models can fit into on-chip SRAM, drastically reducing energy consumption and enabling deployment on mobile devices.
Achieves state-of-the-art compression rates (35x to 49x) on large-scale networks like AlexNet and VGG-16 with no accuracy loss.
